metadata_information.title: Leveraging Women’s Views to Influence Gender Norms around Women Working : Evidence from an Online Intervention in Indonesia
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Leveraging Women’s Views to Influence Gender Norms around Women Working : Evidence from an Online Intervention in Indonesia

document_description.abstract: How to influence social norms that drive behavior in relation to women’s participation in employment is not well understood. Providing randomly selected participants with information on the extent of (i) women’s support for women with children working; (ii) husband’s support for sharing day-to-day childcare with wives; and (iii) mothers’ and mother-in-law’s support for working women, increased the probability of choosing an online career mentoring course for women over a shopping voucher of equal value by 25 percent. Information beyond women’s support for working women further increased support for women working for some groups, although not strongly so.

An objective of the series is to get the findings out quickly, even if the presentations are less than fully polished. The papers carry the names of the authors and should be cited accordingly.
